Identity.Name: StingMGSGasTypes
Identity.Definition: Medical Gas System gas type codes per HTM 02-01 / NFPA 99. Carried as Pset_StingHealthcareMGS.GasType on every medical-gas element (manifolds, VIEs, ZVBs, terminal units, AVSUs). Drives BS 1710 pipe-identification colour, NFPA 99 §5.1.13 diversity factor in MgasFlowSolver, and the 12-step §5.1.12 verification log structure.

Governance.StandardsBasis: HTM 02-01:2006 (UK Medical Gases); NFPA 99:2024 (US Health Care Facilities Code); BS EN ISO 7396-1 (piped medical gas systems)

Value.Definition: Oxygen — medical grade. Pipe ID white. Stored as VIE / cylinder manifold; distributed at 4 bar to terminal units. Most-distributed medical gas.
Nitrous Oxide — anaesthetic. Pipe ID blue. Cylinder-manifold supply; distributed at 4 bar. Restricted to anaesthetic / theatre areas.
Medical Air 4 bar — clean dry compressed air for respiratory therapy. Pipe ID black/white quartered.
Surgical / Instrument Air 7 bar — for powered surgical tools. Pipe ID black/white quartered with "7 bar" marking.
Medical Vacuum — suction. Pipe ID yellow. NFPA 99 / HTM 02-01 verification critical.
Anaesthetic Gas Scavenging System — exhausts waste anaesthetic gas from theatres. Pipe ID purple.
Medical Carbon Dioxide — insufflation gas for laparoscopic surgery. Pipe ID grey. Limited installations (theatre + endoscopy).
Medical Nitrogen — high-pressure powered surgical tools (alternative to AIR-7). Pipe ID black.
Entonox — 50% nitrous oxide / 50% oxygen mix; analgesia for maternity / A&E. Pipe ID white/blue striped.
Heliox — helium/oxygen mix, specialist respiratory support. Pipe ID brown/white striped.
